$comment: USML 2026.05 — canonical backend emission output example. Non-normative; documents the shape of a Level 3 conformant emission per §6.5 Emission attribution metadata. The actual emitted CSS + HTML for button-system lives inline in /02_reference-packs/patterns/button-system/examples/index.html; this document describes what that emission documents about itself when wearing its attribution metadata.

outputs.description: Reference specimen for the button-system pattern. Contains: <style> block with token consumption (124 var(--...) references); variant attribute selectors ([data-intent="primary|secondary|ghost|destructive|link"], [data-size="xs|sm|md|lg"]); microstate selectors (:hover, :focus-visible, [aria-disabled]); slot styling; rendered HTML demonstrating the pattern in use.

anatomyPreservation.evidence: Specimen contains <button class="action-button"> elements demonstrating every mandatory slot from the source Pattern's primitives/index.json.

variantPreservation.evidence: Specimen contains attribute selectors for every variant axis declared in the source Pattern. 124 selectors of form [data-*] confirm queryability.

compositionPreservation.evidence: Specimen consumes tokens-baseline via :root CSS custom properties; no peer-pack contracts are inlined.

accessibilityPreservation.evidence: Specimen contains 36 ARIA attribute references. role="button" and aria-disabled are present where the source's accessibility contract declares them.

tokenConsumption.aestheticPackSwapMechanism: [data-aesthetic="<name>"] selectors per Phase 22.5.A swap demo at /demos/aesthetic-swap/
tokenConsumption.evidence: Specimen reads every token value via var(--token-name). Aesthetic pack at /aesthetics/default/tokens.css supplies values; swap demo exercises runtime swap.

microstates.evidence: Specimen contains 17 microstate selectors covering the full standardized vocabulary for button patterns.

_note: This document is a non-normative description of an emission's shape. A USML Backend Emitter's actual emitted output (CSS, HTML, JSX, etc.) is target-specific and does not match this JSON document literally — this document describes WHAT the emission attests about itself when wearing its §6.5 attribution metadata.
